main.title: Mateus Andrade
main.subtitle: Full Stack Developer
main.description: Mid-level Full Stack Developer shipping end-to-end features in distributed systems — from React Native apps to event-driven ASP.NET Core backends, with real-time voice AI integrations.

about.title: About Me
about.description: I'm Mateus, a mid-level Full Stack Developer with hands-on experience designing and shipping complex end-to-end features — mobile, backend, and dashboard — in distributed systems. Bachelor's in Information Systems from UFOP (2020–2025), I currently work on Woolly, a gamified educational platform with real-time voice AI tutors.
about.description2: My focus areas are event-driven architecture, microservices, performance, and real-time AI integrations. I have a track record of measurable impact: parallelized OpenAI calls reducing quiz generation latency by 70%, designed a multi-platform push notification system with 99 automated tests and 3× throughput, and built idempotent event-driven pipelines with RabbitMQ + MassTransit.
about.description3: I work fluently in C#, TypeScript, Python, and SQL, and I enjoy problems that require design decisions — concurrency, idempotency, multi-tenancy with RLS, observability. Comfortable with agile collaboration, code review, and informal mentoring.

jobs.summary: Gamified educational platform with real-time voice AI tutors. End-to-end work on mobile, distributed backend, dedicated jobs server, admin dashboard, and infrastructure.
items: Architect and develop the React Native/Expo app, the ASP.NET Core backend, and a dedicated Hangfire jobs server on a Linux VPS with Docker, Caddy and Ansible
Reduced AI quiz generation time by 70% (22s → 7s) by parallelizing OpenAI calls with Task.WhenAll and using progressive backoff polling
Designed the gamification event-driven architecture with RabbitMQ + MassTransit, idempotent consumers via Event Store and at-least-once delivery
Built a multi-platform push notification system with 4 time-segmented Hangfire jobs, parallel batch sending via SemaphoreSlim (3× throughput) and 99 automated tests
Integrated real-time voice pipeline (WebRTC + LiveKit + AssemblyAI STT + OpenAI + Google TTS) with contextual continuation between sessions
Modeled a multi-tenant PostgreSQL database with Row Level Security on 9+ tables, 57 versioned Sqitch migrations and partial unique indexes

jobs.summary: Front-end of the City Hall Portal using Next.js (SSR, Dynamic Routing, Revalidation).
items: Led the front-end of the City Hall Portal in Next.js, achieving +30% Lighthouse performance
Built a design system of reusable components following WCAG and optimized technical SEO
Integrated REST APIs and standardized revalidation/SSR for critical content

jobs.summary: End-to-end e-commerce flows and RESTful APIs.
items: Implemented cart, checkout and JWT authentication integrated with payment APIs
Built RESTful APIs in Node.js/Express with PostgreSQL modeling, applying Clean Code

cases.title: Featured Technical Cases
cases.subtitle: Design decisions and measurable impact in production

items.name: AI Quiz Generation Optimization
items.tagline: From 22s to 7s by parallelizing OpenAI calls
items.problem: Generating quizzes via OpenAI took ~22s for 5 questions because the model produced a giant JSON token by token with all options and rationales. Combined with fixed 2s polling and animations forcing minimum wait, UX suffered.
solution: ComputeBatches method splits N questions into batches of 2-3 (e.g. 5→[3,2], 15→[3,3,3,3,3])
Task.WhenAll fires parallel calls with lean prompts and merges results re-numbering IDs
Polling with progressive backoff (1s → 5s, capped) replaces the fixed 2s interval
Animation minimum time aligned with the new parallel generation duration

items.name: Distributed Push Notification System
items.problem: Sending push 1 by 1 to thousands of users hits Expo rate limits. Expired tokens accumulate silently, reminders at the wrong time have low conversion, and production operations need visibility and manual action.
solution: Dedicated Hangfire server on a self-hosted VPS with Docker, Caddy/HTTPS and basic auth
5 recurring jobs and 1 delayed job: pending (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED), streak warning, inactivity warning, daily reminders (4 BRT slots), receipt checking
Parallel batch sending via SemaphoreSlim(3) + ConcurrentBag for thread-safe collection
Batch deactivation of invalid tokens via ExecuteUpdateAsync in a single query
Soft pre-prompt for Apple Review, templates with placeholders and per-user rate limiting
impact: 99 automated tests across 3 projects (Testcontainers + xUnit + Moq)
Reminders segmented by user preference (4 windows)
Invalid tokens deactivated automatically in batch

items.name: Event-Driven Architecture for Gamification
items.tagline: RabbitMQ + MassTransit with idempotent consumers and Event Store
items.problem: Multiple systems react to the same event (level-up triggers missions, collectibles, unlock screen, push). Coupling everything in XpService would create a God Service hard to test and prone to cascade failures.
solution: Immutable events: AlunoSubiuDeNivel, AlunoPromovido, RankingFinalizado, StreakMilestoneAtingido
Consumers in Modules/Gamificacao register in EventStore before processing (idempotency via composite eventId)
At-least-once delivery + automatic retry + publishing failures don't abort the main transaction
Multi-level jumps process intermediate levels without losing rewards
impact: Total decoupling between XP, ranking, collectibles, missions and streaks
Full auditing via custom Event Store

items.name: Real-time Voice Pipeline with AI
items.tagline: WebRTC + LiveKit + STT/LLM/TTS with contextual continuation
items.problem: Voice conversation with AI must be sub-second end-to-end. Resuming an old conversation without context frustrates the student, but sending the full transcript on every call is expensive and slow.
solution: LiveKit SDK in React Native connects to AssemblyAI (STT) → OpenAI → Google TTS pipeline
ContinuationContextBuilder truncates transcript (last 8 turns, 400 chars/msg) on demand
Extracts positives, improvements and a tip from the previous session's structured feedback
Detects level progression and fetches student memory by subject
Shared cache via TanStack Query across pre-call Dialog, call screen, history and feedback
impact: Structured feedback reused with no extra prompt cost
Centralized hook for fetch + 404/network error handling

items.name: Woolly
items.tagline: Gamified educational platform with voice AI tutors
items.description: Educational app where students chat by voice, in real time, with personalized AI agents. It blends pedagogy, gamification (levels, weekly ranking, missions, collectibles, streaks) and advanced conversational tech (WebRTC, STT→LLM→TTS) for immersive learning.
highlights: Distributed ASP.NET Core backend + dedicated Hangfire jobs server
Admin dashboard in React + multi-tenancy with Row Level Security
Self-hosted infra: Docker, Caddy, Ansible, Infisical

items.name: City Hall Portal — Sigesis
items.description: Led the front-end of the City Hall Portal using Next.js with SSR, Dynamic Routing and Revalidation. Built a reusable design system following WCAG and optimized technical SEO.
highlights: REST API integration and strategic revalidation
